capability_objective: Resolve The Quiet Observatory in Central Branch and Cooking with Winter Roots in East Branch with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Cooking with Winter Roots is still on-loan, change only Cooking with Winter Roots from on-loan to on-hold. Leave The Quiet Observatory unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify circulation desk with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. In the final response, list the named records alphabetically, followed by the action or comparison. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two library records in the provided sandbox. Use the genuine local `./library-catalog` executable through Pi's Bash tool for every catalog operation. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or generated data, its operation evidence, or anything under `.harness`; do not edit those files or manufacture evidence.

Find the exact title `The Quiet Observatory` at `Central Branch` and the exact title `Cooking with Winter Roots` at `East Branch`. The two searches are independent. Make them as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct catalog operation per call. Do not combine the searches in one shell command and do not start background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Continue only if both searches each return exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID. Then, in the next assistant action that accesses the catalog, retrieve both complete records as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, one direct get per call, using only the ID returned by that title's own search. Wait for both retrievals. If either search is missing, ambiguous, or lacks a stable ID, stop before every retrieval, mutation, and notice; identify the unresolved lookup without guessing and do not create the report. If either retrieved record does not match its search or lacks a status, stop before every mutation and notice.

Only after both complete records have been retrieved, evaluate the retrieved status of `Cooking with Winter Roots`. If and only if it is exactly `on-loan`, use one later direct catalog operation to change only its returned stable ID to `on-hold`. Treat the mutation as successful only if that operation succeeds and returns the same record with status `on-hold`. If the status is anything else or the mutation fails, make no catalog change and do not send a notice. Leave `The Quiet Observatory`, every same-name or similarly named record, and every other record unchanged. Only after the permitted mutation succeeds, use one still-later direct catalog operation to notify `circulation desk` for the changed record; the executable derives the outcome from the successful change. Do not notify if the mutation was skipped or failed. Do not create, cancel, or perform any other catalog write or notification, and do not invoke any extra catalog operation beyond the two searches, the two eligible gets, the one conditional update, and its one dependent notice.

After the workflow, write `circulation_outcome.md` as the only new deliverable and leave no scratch files. It must contain exactly three nonblank, newline-terminated Markdown bullets with no heading or other prose. List the named records alphabetically, so `Cooking with Winter Roots` is first and `The Quiet Observatory` is second. Shape each record bullet exactly as `- <name> | <stable ID> | <branch> | <final status>`, taking values from its complete record and, for a successful update, its returned updated record. The third bullet must begin `- Action |` when the conditional change succeeds, name the title, old status, new status, and recipient, and state that notification followed the successful update. If the condition is false, begin it `- Comparison |` and state that no update or notice was sent. Include no literal angle brackets. Run `python3 -B .harness/verify.py` after writing the report. Return exactly the report content as the final response.
